rulings.Case Title: NANCY J YEE VS. EDWARD YEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Sanctions, Attorneys Fees And Costs Agaisnt Pltf;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 15, 2013, Line 1, DEFENDANTS EDWARD YEE AND JENNY GRANADOS' Motion For Sanctions, Attorneys Fees And Costs Agaisnt Plaintiff; Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: BYRA MARBLE VS. ORGANON USA, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Organon Usa, Inc., Organon International Inc., Organon Pharmaceuticals Usa Inc., Llc (Fka Organon Pharmaceuticals Usa Inc.), Merck & Co. (Fka Schering Plough Corporation)'S Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Claims For Forum Non Conveniens
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 15, 2013, Line 12, Defendants Organon Usa, Inc., Organon International Inc., Organon Pharmaceuticals Usa Inc., Llc (Fka Organon Pharmaceuticals Usa Inc.), Merck & Co. (Fka Schering Plough Corporation)'S Motion To Dismiss Or Stay Claims For Forum Non Conveniens. The Court denies the motion as to Ms. Byra Marble. She is a California resident and Defendant fails to show that California is a seriously inconvenient forum with regard to her claim. The Court notes that Defendant McKesson is also a California resident. The Court grants the motion and stays the action as to the non-California residents and severs their claims. The torts did not occur in California, the Court can reasonably assume that the sources of proof and physicians remain in the home states based on the Fact Sheets filed in federal court, and California has little interest in adjudicating their claims. The presence of McKesson is less compelling as to these plaintiffs. See Stangvik v. Shiley, Inc., (1991) 54 Cal.3d 744, 756. The granting of the motion is conditioned on defendants agreeing to submit to jurisdiction in the home states of plaintiffs, and defendants continuing to abide by their statement in their moving papers that they agree that the statute of limitations is tolled as of the filing of this lawsuit. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

The Court notes that Defendant McKesson is also a California resident. The Court grants the motion and stays the action as to the non-California residents and severs their claims. The torts did not occur in California, the Court can reasonably assume that the sources of proof and physicians remain in the home states based on the Fact Sheets filed in federal court, and California has little interest in adjudicating their claims. The presence of McKesson is less compelling as to these plaintiffs. See Stangvik v. Shiley, Inc., (1991) 54 Cal.3d 744, 756. The granting of the motion is conditioned on defendants agreeing to submit to jurisdiction in the home states of plaintiffs, and defendants continuing to abide by their statement in their moving papers that they agree that the statute of limitations is tolled as of the filing of this lawsuit. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: SUSAN CHAN VS. DELTA DENTAL OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Petition To Compel Arb; Petition
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 15, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANT DELTA DENTAL OF CALIFORNIA's Petition To Compel arbitration. Granted. Defendant has met its burden of establishing the existence of a written agreement to arbitrate between the parties, although a signed copy of 2006 DeltaCare Network Provider Agreement ("2006 Contract") which contains the relevant arbitration clause is not before the Court. The evidence shows that Plaintiff embraced the 2006 Contract during the life of the contract, including timely demanding arbitration pursuant to §7.4(c) on September 22, 2010 with the AAA. (Phillips Decl., Exhibit A.) Plaintiff's arbitration demand in 2010 is essentially the same dispute that is now the subject of this lawsuit. The circumstantial evidence establishes that Plaintiff knew of the existence of the 2006 Contract (with its arbitration clause) and that she signed it. For example, before this lawsuit was filed, Plaintiff acknowledged that she signed an addendum that required her to sign the 2006 Contract, but she simply did not have a signed copy of the 2006 Contract. (Id. at Exhibit B; Petition, Exhibit B, 9.) The arbitration clause is broadly worded and encompasses Plaintiff's statutory claims. Plaintiff has not shown the 2006 Contract arbitration agreement was unconscionable. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: LEVIN SIMES, LLP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. HERSH & HERSH, A CALIFORNIA PROFESSIONAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 15, 2013, Line 22, DEFENDANTs HERSH & HERSH, NANCY HERSH & MARK BURTON's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Granted as to the second, third and fifth causes of action and denied as to the first and fourth causes of action. The second, third and fifth causes of action arise from protected activity. On the facts of this case, the commercial speech exception in CCP §425.17 clearly does not apply. (See Taheri Law Group v. Evans (2008) 160 Cal.App.4th 482, 489-492.) Plaintiff has failed to show a probability of prevailing because the claims are barred by the litigation privilege. (See Cal. Civ. Code § 47(b).) The first and fourth causes of action do not arise from protected activity because the gravamen of those claims is not the protected speech. Plaintiff's unopposed request for judicial notice is granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: LEVIN SIMES, LLP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. HERSH & HERSH, A CALIFORNIA PROFESSIONAL et al
